title: Cleaning Up the Wye Campaign | Jesse Norman
text_excerpt: Cleaning Up the Wye Campaign | Jesse Norman Skip to main content Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE User account menu Log in Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About In Parliament News Articles Contact Search Cleaning Up the Wye NMITE Cleaning Up the Wye Campaign Phosphate pollution in the River Wye is a very serious issue, destroying the fragile ecosystem and habitats along this gorgeous river, with huge implications for local businesses, tourism and public health. Building on the work of Councillor Elissa Swinglehurst and the Wye and Usk Foundation, I was the first to highlight this issue, in September 2020 , press the agencies for a collective response , and then call for a cross border, all-catchment collective long-term action plan . In June 2021, working with Bill Wiggin MP, Fay Jones MP, Mark Harper MP and David Davies MP, I called the national agencies and the local councils on both sides of the border to a special meeting devoted to this issue. In October 2021 I coordinated a letter from Wye catchment MPs to the Chief Secretary to the Treasury, calling for a specific spending package devoted to the Wye in the 2021 three year Spending Review. The result — finally, lockdowns and other delays notwithstanding — was the Shell Store meeting of July 2022 and the set-up of the Wye Phosphates Working Group, whose terms of reference have now been adopted — very much with my support — by the important Phosphates Commission , established by the four local authorities. Ministers are not able to speak for themselves in Parliament. But as a backbencher, I highlighted the plight of the River Wye in separate Parliamentary Questions to the Prime Minister , the Deputy Prime Minister and the Secretary of State for DEFRA . Among other things, I called for the proceeds of fines on water companies to be ring-fenced and devoted to a new National Rivers Recovery Fund . In November 2022 the Government accepted the ring-fencing , which is a major win. I have also asked the new Office of Environmental Protection to review the situation with the Wye, and expect to meet with them shortly. I finally, have written extensively on this issue, both in the local and national press , including via the links below. Cleaning Up the Wye Campaign Latest News Raising awareness of water pollution 22 August 2025 Local MP Jesse Norman is backing a joint effort by Ross Rowing Club and local firm Haigh Environmental to raise awareness of the issue of water pollution in the River Wye, as well as the solutions available to tackle it. Government action on River Wye “just the first step”, says Jesse 11 March 2025 Local MP and long time River Wye campaigner Jesse Norman has given a qualified welcome to news today of the first joined-up action by governments on the river. Jesse Norman introduces Bill to restore River Wye’s water quality 25 February 2025 Jesse Norman presents a Ten Minute Rule Bill that would require the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs to publish and implement a plan for cleaning and improving the water quality of the River Wye. Solutions needed urgently to save “true national treasure” River Wye from pollution 24 February 2025 The River Wye is one of the most beautiful and ecologically significant rivers in the UK. Jesse Norman calls on Government to reinstate River Wye Action Plan 15 January 2025 Jesse Norman takes the opportunity of a Westminster Hall debate on water quality in rivers, lakes and seas to call on the Government look again at the plan for the River Wye and at least preserve the £35 million of funding pledged by the previous Government to support the restoration of the river. Jesse Norman calls on the Government to back the River Wye Action Plan 8 October 2024 Jesse Norman takes the opportunity of an Opposition Day debate on farming to call on the Government to ensure that the £35 million allocated for the River Wye Action Plan is retained and Rive
